sprint: Welcome-Back Gate -- Constellation Pattern Never Shown
task: A real, user-reported bug with a screenshot: the boot-time 'Welcome back' Identity Gate still rendered a connect-the-dots constellation graphic, even though it was already correctly using the safe decorativeSkyFor() stand-in rather than the real recall pattern. The user's instruction was stronger and simpler: the pattern is never to be shown, not even a decorative stand-in, since a dot-and-line star map reads as 'my secret code is on screen' regardless of which one is actually drawn. Fix: both the single-card gate and the multi-card picker now render a new _buildGateIdentityGlyph() instead of any constellation -- a plain circular glyph showing the Creator's own bonded Story Companion portrait (resolved async via the existing MagicCardArt.resolveCompanionPortrait), falling back to a quiet sparkle glyph while loading or when no portrait is available yet. The two deliberate reveal surfaces (the Awakening ceremony genesis, Magic Card Home's own reveal-gated card) and the header badge's own decorative sky are all untouched. Verified via Playwright (a scoped update to the existing pattern-leak suite, since its old document-wide constellation query was a false positive against the header badge's own separate, correct decorative sky) plus a fresh screenshot at the exact reported viewport confirming zero star pattern remains on this screen, plus full regression across the entire Companion/Magic-Card/Creator Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change; confined to js/magicCardUI.js and css/style.css.
